mechanism_narrative: Rising GDPC1 values combined with steady PAYEMS expansion increases corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in unemployment below the 6.5% threshold signals labor market normalization, reducing recession risk premiums and boosting equity valuations within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.5% and continues declining for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns average negative when UNRATE crosses below 6.5% twice in a year.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPIAUCSL and the federal funds rate compress equity multiples by raising real discount rates and signaling restrictive policy, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise concurrently for three months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and order backlogs, providing immediate positive momentum for industrial and broader equity weights over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no correlation with INDPRO momentum (Pearson r < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in housing starts below 900,000 units indicates weakening consumer demand and construction sector stress, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST drops below 900.0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >50% of instances where HOUST falls below 900 for two months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS values alongside stable or growing PAYEMS values confirms broad-based consumer spending strength, supporting earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative when RSAFS and PAYEMS both increase month-over-month for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.5% and CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0, the Federal Reserve faces a dual mandate conflict that delays policy normalization, increasing volatility and depressing equity valuations over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive when FEDFUNDS > 0.5% and CPIAUCSL > 235.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid job creation combined with a sharp unemployment decline signals an overheating labor market that accelerates wage-driven inflation expectations, prompting rate hike fears and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 200000 for three consecutive months while UNRATE falls > 0.2pp
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive when PAYEMS rises >200000 for three months and UNRATE falls >0.2pp.
